channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 4, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Map: 6.0-Magnitude Earthquake Shakes the Philippines
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘encroach’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 36 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: La minería ilegal de minerales críticos depreda la Amazonía
item.description: La demanda global de minerales críticos, que se usan para fabricar drones y vehículos eléctricos, está en aumento, lo que ha llevado una nueva ola de delincuencia a la selva tropical más grande del mundo.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova

media:content.media:credit: Eraldo Peres/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Una mina en Minaçu, en el estado de Goiás, Brasil, que produce elementos de tierras raras como neodimio, praseodimio, terbio y disprosio.

item.title: La Gala del Met 2026: tema, código de vestimenta y todo lo que debes saber
item.description: Una de las presencias más esperadas es la de Beyoncé. Es copresidenta junto con Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams y la directora de contenidos de Condé Nast, Anna Wintour.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Damon Winter/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Beyoncé arriving to the Met Gala in 2016, the last year she attended the event.

item.title: Word of the Day: encroach
item.description: This word has appeared in 36 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ‘Lord of the Flies,’ and 7 More Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new adaptation of the 1954 novel comes to Netflix, and “Outlander” wraps up its eight-season run.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: via Netflix
media:content.media:description: This four-episode iteration of “The Lord of the Flies” was first released in Britain on BBC.

item.title: Operation Epic Fury, Meet Operation Colossal Blunder
item.description: With the threat of a future closing of the Strait of Hormuz a drone strike away, the Iranians appear to actually hold the cards.
item.dc:creator: Scott Anderson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Nathan Howard for The New York Times

item.title: Los autos eléctricos ganan terreno frente a la volatilidad del petróleo
item.description: En Costa Rica y otros países, más personas compran vehículos eléctricos para evitar el impacto de los precios del combustible.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing and Tony Cenicola
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Guangzhou Xiaopeng Motors Technology Co Ltd (Xpeng Motors)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: Auto Mercado, una cadena de supermercados, ha recortado entre un 5 y un 10 por ciento el costo de hacer entregas en línea cambiando a furgonetas eléctricas.

item.title: ‘City of Glass’ Is Under Threat From Iran War
item.description: In India, Firozabad has depended on its glassmaking industry for centuries. Fuel prices are starting to shatter businesses already under pressure.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli, Suhasini Raj and Atul Loke

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Female Trainer and a Back-of-the-Pack Horse Create a Historic Day
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, May 4, 2026.

item.title: School Cellphone Ban Study Finds Mixed Results
item.description: Cellphone bans got devices out of students’ hands, according to the first large study. But behavior and academics have not improved, at least so far.
item.dc:creator: Dana Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Lea Suzuki/San Francisco Chronicle, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Cellphone use declined in schools that adopted strict bans, though test scores did not immediately improve.

item.title: Chizi, Standup Comic Exiled in China, Wants to Be More Than Just ‘a Rebel Comedian’
item.description: Having gotten into trouble for making jokes critical of the Chinese government, the standup comic Chizi now lives in self-imposed exile. He’s finding that freedom imposes its own constraints.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan and Chang W. Lee

media:content.media:description: Chizi performed for the first time in years in Tokyo in April. The audience went to see a rebel.

item.title: How the Kremlin Lures Africans Into Russia’s War in Ukraine
item.description: A growing number of men across the continent say they are being promised jobs in Russia, only to be forced into the war. Some go as mercenaries, but many more are drawn unwittingly.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg, John Eligon, Zimasa Matiwane and Ed Ram

media:content.media:credit: Ed Ram for The New York Times

item.title: Why Are Africans Fighting in Russia’s War in Ukraine?
item.description: Some see a clear explanation: Russia needs more troops, and young men in Africa desperately need jobs.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: Center for Strategic and International Studies

media:content.media:credit: Ed Ram for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Family members of people recruited by Russia for the war in Ukraine protested in Nairobi, Kenya, in March. The lack of information on missing loved ones spurred their appeal to lawmakers.

item.title: Can a Great American Songwriter Master the Mini Crossword?
item.description: Yes. In fact, four of them can.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

media:content.media:credit: Photographs by Stefan Ruiz, photo illustration by John Atashian/Getty Images

item.title: No Corrections: May 4, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, May 4, 2026.

item.title: En Costa Rica, conducir un coche eléctrico no es muy complicado
item.description: Costa Rica ha hecho más que la mayoría de los países para promover los vehículos eléctricos y casi uno de cada cinco coches nuevos que se venden es eléctrico.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing

media:content.media:credit: Tony Cenicola/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Utilizar las estaciones de carga puede ser complicado porque los coches eléctricos chinos utilizan enchufes diferentes de los occidentales.

item.title: Mariachi Brothers Detained by ICE Invited to Open for Kacey Musgraves After Release
item.description: The teenagers, who had faced deportation after a high-profile detention, took the stage on Sunday thanks to an invitation from one of country music’s biggest stars.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Luis Antonio Gámez-Cuéllar wiped a tear from his face after he and his family were released from the Dilley Immigration Processing Center in Dilley, Texas, in March.

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 7 Recap: Anywhere Else
item.description: Valerie gets answers about the leak. Mark gets a troubling tattoo.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray

media:content.media:credit: Erin Simkin/HBO
media:content.media:description: The constant chuckling and deflections can make Valerie (Lisa Kudrow) seem out of touch, but she moves on very well from public embarrassment.

item.title: 10-Minute Challenge: ‘Cityscape’ by Romare Bearden
item.description: We’d like you to look at one piece of art for 10 minutes, uninterrupted.
item.dc:creator: Larry Buchanan

item.title: Trump se enfrenta a la realidad de la guerra en Irán
item.description: Las predicciones del presidente Trump de un conflicto relativamente corto con consecuencias económicas mínimas parecen desmoronarse.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Plaza Valiasr, donde una nueva valla publicitaria retrata el cierre del estrecho de Ormuz.

item.title: Tractor-Trailer and Light Post Damaged as Plane Lands at Newark Airport
item.description: The tractor-trailer was on the New Jersey Turnpike on Sunday when it was damaged, the authorities said. Its driver had minor injuries and no one aboard the plane was hurt.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

item.title: Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Florida in ‘Critical Condition’
item.description: A spokesman for the former mayor of New York City said Mr. Giuliani was critical but stable.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rudy Giuliani, the former mayor of New York, is critically ill in a Florida hospital.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Will Help Stranded Ships Leave Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump offered few details on how the new program, which he called Project Freedom, would work, but said interference would be dealt with “forcefully.”
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, David E. Sanger and Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, near Musandam, Oman, on Friday.

item.title: United Plane Strikes Light Pole While Landing
item.description: A United Airlines flight hit a light pole as it landed at Newark Liberty International Airport on Sunday, the Federal Aviation Administration said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

item.title: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
item.description: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A government-organized march in Tehran on Wednesday in support of Iran’s new supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: What Is Hantavirus, Which Is Linked to the Deaths of 3 People Aboard a Cruise Ship?
item.description: Three people aboard a cruise ship sailing the Atlantic Ocean have died from what health officials suspect were hantavirus infections.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka, Hank Sanders and Michael Levenson

media:content.media:credit: BSIP/UIG Via Getty Images)
media:content.media:description: Hantavirus most often spreads from dried rodent droppings.

item.title: Oil Prices Jump While Stock Futures Inch Up
item.description: Oil prices fell and futures on the S&P 500 rose slightly on Sunday as investors reacted to continued uncertainty about the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Charles Krupa/Associated Press

item.title: 8 Arrested at Protest Over ICE Detention Outside Brooklyn Hospital
item.description: Protesters clashed with the police outside Wyckoff Heights Medical Center, where federal immigration agents brought a detainee for evaluation and later dragged him to a waiting car.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius and Dana Rubinstein

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Protests erupted outside Wyckoff Heights Medical Center late Saturday after federal agents brought a detained man in.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 4, 2026
item.description: Kate Schutzengel’s puzzle is for the romantics.
item.dc:creator: Alex Barron

media:content.media:credit: Kristian Thacker for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Get cozy, grab a pencil (or pen) and solve this puzzle.

item.title: The Widow of the Firefighter Slain by a Would-Be Assassin Speaks Out
item.description: The conspiracy theories around the Butler, Pa., assassination attempt hurt Helen Comperatore, but in this age of distrust, partisanship and social media, she nurtures a conspiracy theory of her own.
item.dc:creator: Kate Zernike

media:content.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times

item.title: DeepSeek’s Sequel
item.description: China made the inner workings of the A.I. model open to all, and that’s starting to look like a soft-power win.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times

item.title: 3 Dead in Hantavirus Outbreak Aboard Cruise Ship, W.H.O. Says
item.description: One case of hantavirus infection has been confirmed in a laboratory, the organization said on Sunday. There are five additional suspected cases.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri and Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The cruise ship MV Hondius off Cape Verde on Sunday. Officials identified the ship as the one with passengers infected by hantavirus.

item.title: E.E.O.C. Investigating Discrimination Claim at The New York Times
item.description: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission recently referred a reverse-discrimination complaint by an employee to the agency’s legal unit for review.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Davis O’Brien

media:content.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission is investigating a reverse-discrimination claim filed against The New York Times by a white male employee.

item.title: Jack Thornell, Pulitzer Winning A.P. Photographer, Dies
item.description: He captured one of the most wrenching moments of the civil rights struggle: the agony of James Meredith after he was shot on a Mississippi highway.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter

media:content.media:credit: Jack Thornell/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Jack Thornhill’s Pulitzer Prize-winning photo showing the civil rights activist James Meredith on the ground after being shot in Hernando, Miss., on June 6, 1966. He is looking at the man who shot him, hiding in the woods.

item.title: ‘By the Wind Sailors’ Invade California Beaches by the Thousands
item.description: The brightly colored jellylike marine life, called “Velella velella,” show up every few years when strong winds blow in the right direction, scientists said.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao

media:content.media:credit: Aidin Vaziri/San Francisco Chronicle
media:content.media:description: Velella velella washed up on Baker Beach in San Francisco, Calif., in April.

item.title: Inquiry Into Fed Chair Jerome Powell Could Be Resurrected, Jeanine Pirro Says
item.description: Jeanine Pirro, the U.S. attorney in Washington, dropped an inquiry into Jerome Powell last month after a federal judge blocked her grand jury subpoenas.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve chair, has resisted President Trump’s demands that the central bank slash interest rates.

item.title: ‘Devil Wears Prada’ Sequel Leads Box Office With $77 Million
item.description: It was the biggest opening weekend for a traditional comedy in 11 years. One analyst called the debut “sensational.”
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Spirit Airlines’ Demise Could Help Other Airlines
item.description: Even in its reduced state, the company played an important role in forcing other airlines to keep fares low, some experts said.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi

media:content.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Spirit Airlines’ presence at an airport usually helped to push down the fares of all airlines operating there.

item.title: Vogue Alumni Stage a Reunion for ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’
item.description: A gaggle of former and current Vogue employees convened for a Saturday night screening of “The Devil Wears Prada 2.” The dress code for the evening? Interview chic.
item.dc:creator: Sandra E. Garcia

media:content.media:credit: Emma Rose Milligan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Vogue alumnae caught an opening-weekend showing of “The Devil Wears Prada 2” in Battery Park City in Manhattan on Saturday.

item.title: In a Reversal, Doctors From Countries Under Trump’s Travel Ban Are Allowed to Stay in U.S.
item.description: In a reversal, the Trump administration has exempted foreign physicians from a visa application freeze that was pushing many out of jobs in underserved areas.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan

media:content.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ezequiel Veliz, a family doctor from Venezuela, was detained by federal agents when he fell out of legal status because his new visa application had not been processed. He was released 10 days later.

item.title: Speedy, Easy Roasted Fish With Leeks and Olive Salsa Verde
item.description: Not a fan of removing olive pits? Don’t worry, I have a trick to share with you.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Clark

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Testani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Monica Pierini.
media:content.media:description: Melissa Clark’s roasted fish with leeks and olive salsa verde.

item.title: Blanche Says Others Who Post ‘86 47’ Message Won’t Be Charged Like Comey
item.description: The acting attorney general said such messages were “posted constantly” and said the Justice Department had gathered other evidence against the former F.B.I. director, but declined to describe it.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Nathan Howard/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, at the Justice Department last week.

item.title: OPEC Plus, in Symbolic Gesture, to Increase Oil Production
item.description: The announcement came days after the United Arab Emirates withdrew from the group. The higher output will have little effect on global supplies.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries

media:content.media:credit: Christian Bruna/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The OPEC headquarters in Vienna. Seven of the members of the consortium of oil producing nations on Sunday noted “the importance of adopting a cautious approach.”

item.title: Jeeves and Ask.com Shut Down After Almost 30 Years
item.description: The pioneering search engine shut down on May 1, after nearly 30 years in operation.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins

media:content.media:credit: Newscast/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Ask.com search engine website, with the illustration of the character Jeeves.

item.title: 2 U.S. Service Members Are Missing After Military Exercise in Morocco
item.description: The two Army soldiers were reported missing during a joint military exercise on Saturday near Cap Draa Training Area, a coastal area in southwestern Morocco.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Mosa'Ab Elshamy/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A United States and Moroccan military exercise in Morocco in 2024.

item.title: ‘Wartime Relic’ Explodes Under Campfire in Austria, Injuring Five Children
item.description: The children, aged 10 to 14, were taken to a hospital for treatment after the explosion in Upper Austria, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands

item.title: Posting the Ten Commandments in Classrooms
item.description: Readers discuss a Texas law requiring the display of the Ten Commandments in classrooms. Also: A job seeker’s tale; long movies.

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
